TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr_the man from china_Pani
TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @article{sung2008developing,title={Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages.},author={Sung, Li-May and Lily, I and Hsieh, Fuhui and Lin, Zhemin and others},journal={Taiwan Journal of Linguistics},volume={6},number={2},year={2008}}

S.FORM: teku tumatuturu poisua tacin niaranai tauruu kocisinse.
S.PHON: teku tumatutur~ɾu poisua taʦin niar~ɾanai taur~ɾuu koʦisinse.
S.FORM: teku tumatuturu poisua tacin niaranai tauruu kocisinse.
S.PHON: teku tumatutur~ɾu poisua taʦin niar~ɾanai taur~ɾuu koʦisinse.
S.TRANSL: 我要說關於一位來自大陸的校長.
I'm going to talk about a principal that comes from China.

S.FORM: manasʉ na minkuo masʉpatʉn urima nukai unʉm.
S.PHON: manasɨ na minkuo masɨpatɨn ur~ɾima nukai unɨm.
S.FORM: manasʉ na minkuo masʉpatʉn urima nukai unʉm.
S.PHON: manasɨ na minkuo masɨpatɨn ur~ɾima nukai unɨm.
S.TRANSL: 大約在民國四十五年或者四十六年.
It was in about Minkuo 45th or 46th year.

S.FORM: mesua tanasʉnmia namasia mesua ia sanminsiang kisʉʉn.
S.PHON: mesua tanasɨnmia namasia mesua ia sanminsiaŋ kisɨɨn.
S.FORM: mesua tanasʉnmia namasia mesua ia sanminsiang kisʉʉn.
S.PHON: mesua tanasɨnmia namasia mesua ia sanminsiaŋ kisɨɨn.
S.TRANSL: 當時我們的部落納瑪夏被稱為三民鄉.
At that time, our tribe Namaasia was called Sanminsiang.

S.FORM: 'akia pakangvang tanasa mocaca cau mesua na noten mocaca mokusa maraa aracen makai kasanpo aupoo 'uru kʉkʉ kangvang mokusa 'inia.
S.PHON: ʔakia pakaŋvaŋ tanasa moʦaʦa ʦau mesua na noten moʦaʦa mokusa mar~ɾaa ar~ɾaʦen makai kasanpo aupoo ʔur~ɾu kɨkɨ kaŋvaŋ mokusa ʔinia.
S.FORM: 'akia pakangvang tanasa mocaca cau mesua na noten mocaca mokusa maraa aracen makai kasanpo aupoo 'uru kʉkʉ kangvang mokusa 'inia.
S.PHON: ʔakia pakaŋvaŋ tanasa moʦaʦa ʦau mesua na noten moʦaʦa mokusa mar~ɾaa ar~ɾaʦen makai kasanpo aupoo ʔur~ɾu kɨkɨ kaŋvaŋ mokusa ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 都還沒有車子,當時的人如果要走去遠的地方,比如甲仙埔、大埔,都要徒步去那裏.
There wasn't any car. If people wanted to walk to the place that was far away such as Kasanpo and Aupoo, they would go there on foot.

S.FORM: 'akia tenki kangvang siarupaca sinʉng po'ocipi 'urupaca kangvang karu to'omang.
S.PHON: ʔakia tenki kaŋvaŋ siar~ɾupaʦa sinɨŋ poʔoʦipi ʔur~ɾupaʦa kaŋvaŋ kar~ɾu toʔomaŋ.
S.FORM: 'akia tenki kangvang siarupaca sinʉng po'ocipi 'urupaca kangvang karu to'omang.
S.PHON: ʔakia tenki kaŋvaŋ siar~ɾupaʦa sinɨŋ poʔoʦipi ʔur~ɾupaʦa kaŋvaŋ kar~ɾu toʔomaŋ.
S.TRANSL: 沒有電器,所有使用的光、烹煮都要使用木材或者是竹子.
there wasn't electricity. All the light used and cooking needed to use timber and bamboo.

S.FORM: 'akia kangvang 'akia cucumeen kangvang sisiarupacaa.
S.PHON: ʔakia kaŋvaŋ ʔakia ʦuʦumeen kaŋvaŋ sisiar~ɾupaʦaa.
S.FORM: 'akia kangvang 'akia cucumeen kangvang sisiarupacaa.
S.PHON: ʔakia kaŋvaŋ ʔakia ʦuʦumeen kaŋvaŋ sisiar~ɾupaʦaa.
S.TRANSL: 沒有任何其他的可以使用.

TRANSL.notes: There wasn't any other (tool) that could be used.

S.TRANSL: There wasn't any other that could be used.

S.FORM: mesia cau na Namasia Kanakanavu ia matʉtʉa kangvang maraa muru'inasʉ.
S.PHON: mesia ʦau na namasia kanakanavu ia matɨtɨa kaŋvaŋ mar~ɾaa mur~ɾuʔinasɨ.
S.FORM: mesia cau na Namasia Kanakanavu ia matʉtʉa kangvang maraa muru'inasʉ.
S.PHON: mesia ʦau na namasia kanakanavu ia matɨtɨa kaŋvaŋ mar~ɾaa mur~ɾuʔinasɨ.
S.TRANSL: 當時住在那瑪夏的Kanakanavu族人,都一樣比較艱困.
The Kanakanavu living in Namasia at that time were the same more industrious.

S.FORM: imo maraa ka'an kim cumacʉ'ʉra cau cucumen.
S.PHON: imo mar~ɾaa kaʔan kim ʦumaʦɨʔɨr~ɾa ʦau ʦuʦumen.
S.FORM: imo maraa ka'an kim cumacʉ'ʉra cau cucumen.
S.PHON: imo mar~ɾaa kaʔan kim ʦumaʦɨʔɨr~ɾa ʦau ʦuʦumen.
S.TRANSL: 所以我們比較看不到其他的人.

TRANSL.notes: So, we relatively couldn't see other people (from other tribes).

S.TRANSL: So, we relatively couldn't see other people .

S.FORM: matʉa sii aracen cucumen ka'an kangvangkim mokusa na aracen manamanarʉ.
S.PHON: matɨa sii ar~ɾaʦen ʦuʦumen kaʔan kaŋvaŋkim mokusa na ar~ɾaʦen manamanar~ɾɨ.
S.FORM: matʉa sii aracen cucumen ka'an kangvangkim mokusa na aracen manamanarʉ.
S.PHON: matɨa sii ar~ɾaʦen ʦuʦumen kaʔan kaŋvaŋkim mokusa na ar~ɾaʦen manamanar~ɾɨ.
S.TRANSL: 同樣地,因為很遠,我們也不去比較遠的平地.
Similarly, we wouldn't go to the farther level ground either because it was far away.

S.FORM: surumo 'una can taniara nivatu tacin nanmarua ka'an tavarʉ'ʉ masii Kanakanavu.
S.PHON: sur~ɾumo ʔuna ʦan taniar~ɾa nivatu taʦin nanmar~ɾua kaʔan tavar~ɾɨʔɨ masii kanakanavu.
S.FORM: surumo 'una can taniara nivatu tacin nanmarua ka'an tavarʉ'ʉ masii Kanakanavu.
S.PHON: sur~ɾumo ʔuna ʦan taniar~ɾa nivatu taʦin nanmar~ɾua kaʔan tavar~ɾɨʔɨ masii kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 突然有一天,來了一位年輕人,不會說Kankanavu族語.
One day, there suddenly came a young man who couldn't speak Kanakanavu.

S.FORM: mesua mamarangmia ia maraa ka'an pa tavarʉ'ʉ masii nakasaiana.
S.PHON: mesua mamar~ɾaŋmia ia mar~ɾaa kaʔan pa tavar~ɾɨʔɨ masii nakasaiana.
S.FORM: mesua mamarangmia ia maraa ka'an pa tavarʉ'ʉ masii nakasaiana.
S.PHON: mesua mamar~ɾaŋmia ia mar~ɾaa kaʔan pa tavar~ɾɨʔɨ masii nakasaiana.
S.TRANSL: 當時我們的長輩們還不說國語.
At that time, our seniors were still not able to speak Mandarin.

S.FORM: kacaua pa cau mesua masii ripun.
S.PHON: kaʦaua pa ʦau mesua masii r~ɾipun.
S.FORM: kacaua pa cau mesua masii ripun.
S.PHON: kaʦaua pa ʦau mesua masii r~ɾipun.
S.TRANSL: 當時還有很多人說日語.
There were still many people speaking Japanese at that time.

S.FORM: cumacʉ'ʉra namarua isua iovutu.
S.PHON: ʦumaʦɨʔɨr~ɾa namar~ɾua isua iovutu.
S.FORM: cumacʉ'ʉra namarua isua iovutu.
S.PHON: ʦumaʦɨʔɨr~ɾa namar~ɾua isua iovutu.
S.TRANSL: 看到那位年輕人來.
saw the young man come.

S.FORM: pasin mamarangmia niaranaikasunan iovutu kisʉʉn.
S.PHON: pasin mamar~ɾaŋmia niar~ɾanaikasunan iovutu kisɨɨn.
S.FORM: pasin mamarangmia niaranaikasunan iovutu kisʉʉn.
S.PHON: pasin mamar~ɾaŋmia niar~ɾanaikasunan iovutu kisɨɨn.
S.TRANSL: 我們的長輩們對他說:「你是從哪裡來的?」
Our seniors said to him, "Where do you come from?"

S.FORM: marivari nguain makasi niaranaiku tauru.
S.PHON: mar~ɾivar~ɾi ŋuain makasi niar~ɾanaiku taur~ɾu.
S.FORM: marivari nguain makasi niaranaiku tauru.
S.PHON: mar~ɾivar~ɾi ŋuain makasi niar~ɾanaiku taur~ɾu.
S.TRANSL: 他回答說「我來自大陸.」
He replied, "I come from China."

S.FORM: mesua cʉ'ʉrʉn kan mamarangmia ia nguain tamna cenana manasʉ mapusan uturu nukai usʉpatʉ.
S.PHON: mesua ʦɨʔɨr~ɾɨn kan mamar~ɾaŋmia ia ŋuain tamna ʦenana manasɨ mapusan utur~ɾu nukai usɨpatɨ.
S.FORM: mesua cʉ'ʉrʉn kan mamarangmia ia nguain tamna cenana manasʉ mapusan uturu nukai usʉpatʉ.
S.PHON: mesua ʦɨʔɨr~ɾɨn kan mamar~ɾaŋmia ia ŋuain tamna ʦenana manasɨ mapusan utur~ɾu nukai usɨpatɨ.
S.TRANSL: 據說當時長輩們看著他的年紀大約二十三或者二十四歲.
It is said that the seniors saw that he was at about the age of 23 or 24 at that time.

S.FORM: arakicacʉ mamarangmia sii tavarʉ'ʉ masii ripun arakukukuncu kanakanavu.
S.PHON: ar~ɾakiʦaʦɨ mamar~ɾaŋmia sii tavar~ɾɨʔɨ masii r~ɾipun ar~ɾakukukunʦu kanakanavu.
S.FORM: arakicacʉ mamarangmia sii tavarʉ'ʉ masii ripun arakukukuncu kanakanavu.
S.PHON: ar~ɾakiʦaʦɨ mamar~ɾaŋmia sii tavar~ɾɨʔɨ masii r~ɾipun ar~ɾakukukunʦu kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 我們長輩嚇了一跳,因為他竟然會說日語,就和Kanakanavu族人相處在一起了

TRANSL.notes: Our seniors were too surprised because he was unexpectedly able to speak Japanese. And so (he) got along with the Kanakanavu people.

S.TRANSL: Our seniors were too surprised because he was unexpectedly able to speak Japanese. And so got along with the Kanakanavu people.

S.FORM: tumatuturucu nguain makasi tekan mon na minshengguoxiao toisua kocisinse.
S.PHON: tumatutur~ɾuʦu ŋuain makasi tekan mon na minsheŋ*uo*iao toisua koʦisinse.
S.FORM: tumatuturucu nguain makasi tekan mon na minshengguoxiao toisua kocisinse.
S.PHON: tumatutur~ɾuʦu ŋuain makasi tekan mon na minsheŋ*uo*iao toisua koʦisinse.
S.TRANSL: 他就說要待在民生國小當校長.
He said that he would stay at Minsheng Elementary School as the principal.

S.FORM: sua kocisinse isi ia kari mamarangmia masii ripun cucuru kari ʉngcoo nakasaiana ia xiaozhang kisʉʉn.
S.PHON: sua koʦisinse isi ia kar~ɾi mamar~ɾaŋmia masii r~ɾipun ʦuʦur~ɾu kar~ɾi ɨŋʦoo nakasaiana ia *iao*haŋ kisɨɨn.
S.FORM: sua kocisinse isi ia kari mamarangmia masii ripun cucuru kari ʉngcoo nakasaiana ia xiaozhang kisʉʉn.
S.PHON: sua koʦisinse isi ia kar~ɾi mamar~ɾaŋmia masii r~ɾipun ʦuʦur~ɾu kar~ɾi ɨŋʦoo nakasaiana ia *iao*haŋ kisɨɨn.
S.TRANSL: "Kocisinse"這句話是我們長輩用日語說的,中文真正的說法是「校長」.
The word, "kocisinse," is how our seniors said in Japanese. The correct way to say in Mandarin is "xiaozhang."

S.FORM: mesua tati'ing pakim ka'ankim tavarʉ'ʉ ah nen tia vu vuren sua marisinatʉ misee sii cucuru 'akia tavara'amia.
S.PHON: mesua tatiʔiŋ pakim kaʔankim tavar~ɾɨʔɨ ah nen tia vu vur~ɾen sua mar~ɾisinatɨ misee sii ʦuʦur~ɾu ʔakia tavar~ɾaʔamia.
S.FORM: mesua tati'ing pakim ka'ankim tavarʉ'ʉ ah nen tia vu vuren sua marisinatʉ misee sii cucuru 'akia tavara'amia.
S.PHON: mesua tatiʔiŋ pakim kaʔankim tavar~ɾɨʔɨ ah nen tia vu vur~ɾen sua mar~ɾisinatɨ misee sii ʦuʦur~ɾu ʔakia tavar~ɾaʔamia.
S.TRANSL: 當時我們的年紀還很小,不知道讀書會有什麼成果,因為的確我們懂的不多.
At that time, we were still too young, not knowing what achievement we could have from studying because what we know was surely little.

S.FORM: ka'ankim tavarʉ'ʉ marisinatʉ nakasaiana ka'ankim tavarʉ'ʉ pokarikari.
S.PHON: kaʔankim tavar~ɾɨʔɨ mar~ɾisinatɨ nakasaiana kaʔankim tavar~ɾɨʔɨ pokar~ɾikar~ɾi.
S.FORM: ka'ankim tavarʉ'ʉ marisinatʉ nakasaiana ka'ankim tavarʉ'ʉ pokarikari.
S.PHON: kaʔankim tavar~ɾɨʔɨ mar~ɾisinatɨ nakasaiana kaʔankim tavar~ɾɨʔɨ pokar~ɾikar~ɾi.
S.TRANSL: 我們不會寫跟說中文.
We could not write and speak Mandarin.

S.FORM: tenoman mokusa na tarisinata mesua cʉpʉngmia.
S.PHON: tenoman mokusa na tar~ɾisinata mesua ʦɨpɨŋmia.
S.FORM: tenoman mokusa na tarisinata mesua cʉpʉngmia.
S.PHON: tenoman mokusa na tar~ɾisinata mesua ʦɨpɨŋmia.
S.TRANSL: 當時我們在想「去學校有什麼用?」
At that time, we were thinking, "What is it for to go to school?"

S.FORM: nakai iovutucu namaraa isua tumatuturu kimia sua nganaini kan ia l2Mzhangjie kisʉʉn niarainai na tauru.
S.PHON: nakai iovutuʦu namar~ɾaa isua tumatutur~ɾu kimia sua ŋanaini kan ia **m*haŋjie kisɨɨn niar~ɾainai na taur~ɾu.
S.FORM: nakai iovutucu namaraa isua tumatuturu kimia sua nganaini kan ia l2Mzhangjie kisʉʉn niarainai na tauru.
S.PHON: nakai iovutuʦu namar~ɾaa isua tumatutur~ɾu kimia sua ŋanaini kan ia **m*haŋjie kisɨɨn niar~ɾainai na taur~ɾu.
S.TRANSL: 但是那位年輕人來了之後,告訴我們說他的名字叫張傑,來自大陸.
But after that young man came, he told us that his name was Jhangjie and that he came from China.

S.FORM: makasuacu mon nguain na tarisinata si'icʉpʉ paira mocʉmcʉm aramia'ʉ.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu mon ŋuain na tar~ɾisinata siʔiʦɨpɨ pair~ɾa moʦɨmʦɨm ar~ɾamiaʔɨ.
S.FORM: makasuacu mon nguain na tarisinata si'icʉpʉ paira mocʉmcʉm aramia'ʉ.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu mon ŋuain na tar~ɾisinata siʔiʦɨpɨ pair~ɾa moʦɨmʦɨm ar~ɾamiaʔɨ.
S.TRANSL: 之後,他住在學校,每天很早起床.
afterwards, he lived in the school and woke up early every day.

S.FORM: takacicin nguain marisiki mo'uru'uru ngʉcoo pangka.
S.PHON: takaʦiʦin ŋuain mar~ɾisiki moʔur~ɾuʔur~ɾu ŋɨʦoo paŋka.
S.FORM: takacicin nguain marisiki mo'uru'uru ngʉcoo pangka.
S.PHON: takaʦiʦin ŋuain mar~ɾisiki moʔur~ɾuʔur~ɾu ŋɨʦoo paŋka.
S.TRANSL: 每天自己打掃、擦桌子.
He cleaned up and wiped tables every day.

S.FORM: patimanʉngʉnkee sua tia to'onaa a seto marisinitʉ.
S.PHON: patimanɨŋɨnkee sua tia toʔonaa a seto mar~ɾisinitɨ.
S.FORM: patimanʉngʉnkee sua tia to'onaa a seto marisinitʉ.
S.PHON: patimanɨŋɨnkee sua tia toʔonaa a seto mar~ɾisinitɨ.
S.TRANSL: 他把學生要讀書的地方整理得好好的.
He put away all the things in the classroom.

S.FORM: turupangcu marisiki mokusa na si'inʉva tarisinatʉ maritarʉ tecu iovutu seto.
S.PHON: tur~ɾupaŋʦu mar~ɾisiki mokusa na siʔinɨva tar~ɾisinatɨ mar~ɾitar~ɾɨ teʦu iovutu seto.
S.FORM: turupangcu marisiki mokusa na si'inʉva tarisinatʉ maritarʉ tecu iovutu seto.
S.PHON: tur~ɾupaŋʦu mar~ɾisiki mokusa na siʔinɨva tar~ɾisinatɨ mar~ɾitar~ɾɨ teʦu iovutu seto.
S.TRANSL: 打掃完後,就去校門口,等待學生要來了.
After cleaning up, he would go to the school gate and waited for the students to come.

S.FORM: noo cumacʉ'ʉra na seto mokusa ana ngʉcoo si'inʉva ia manasʉ kariin ia siaupʉngiu cau misee musu'ʉvʉ kamua.
S.PHON: noo ʦumaʦɨʔɨr~ɾa na seto mokusa ana ŋɨʦoo siʔinɨva ia manasɨ kar~ɾiin ia siaupɨŋiu ʦau misee musuʔɨvɨ kamua.
S.FORM: noo cumacʉ'ʉra na seto mokusa ana ngʉcoo si'inʉva ia manasʉ kariin ia siaupʉngiu cau misee musu'ʉvʉ kamua.
S.PHON: noo ʦumaʦɨʔɨr~ɾa na seto mokusa ana ŋɨʦoo siʔinɨva ia manasɨ kar~ɾiin ia siaupɨŋiu ʦau misee musuʔɨvɨ kamua.

TRANSL.notes: 如果看到學生在校門口,通常他會說:「小朋友早(中文),早安.」

S.TRANSL: 如果看到學生在校門口,通常他會說:「小朋友早 ,早安.」
If he saw student at the school gate, he would usually say "Siaopengyou zao. Musu'ʉvʉ kamua."

S.FORM: sii mesua ka'anpa nguain tavarʉ'ʉ masii Kanakanavu.
S.PHON: sii mesua kaʔanpa ŋuain tavar~ɾɨʔɨ masii kanakanavu.
S.FORM: sii mesua ka'anpa nguain tavarʉ'ʉ masii Kanakanavu.
S.PHON: sii mesua kaʔanpa ŋuain tavar~ɾɨʔɨ masii kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 因為那個時候他還不會說卡那卡那富語.
Because he was still not able to speak Kanakanavu at that time.

S.FORM: ketuturu nen amusenkamu noo cauan misee.
S.PHON: ketutur~ɾu nen amusenkamu noo ʦauan misee.
S.FORM: ketuturu nen amusenkamu noo cauan misee.
S.PHON: ketutur~ɾu nen amusenkamu noo ʦauan misee.
S.TRANSL: 如果你們說「早安」要怎麼說?

TRANSL.notes: If you say "Good morning," how (can I) say it?

S.TRANSL: If you say "Good morning," how say it?

S.FORM: tuturuʉnmia musu'ʉvʉ tasiarʉ kisʉʉnmia takutavarʉ'ʉcu.
S.PHON: tutur~ɾuɨnmia musuʔɨvɨ tasiar~ɾɨ kisɨɨnmia takutavar~ɾɨʔɨʦu.
S.FORM: tuturuʉnmia musu'ʉvʉ tasiarʉ kisʉʉnmia takutavarʉ'ʉcu.
S.PHON: tutur~ɾuɨnmia musuʔɨvɨ tasiar~ɾɨ kisɨɨnmia takutavar~ɾɨʔɨʦu.
S.TRANSL: 我們教他"musu'ʉvʉ tasiarʉ",就學會了.
We taught him "musu'ʉvʉ tasiarʉ" and then he learned it.

S.FORM: noo cumacʉ'ra kimia ia totasiarʉ ia makahicu musu'ʉvʉ tasiarʉ misee kimia.
S.PHON: noo ʦumaʦɨʔr~ɾa kimia ia totasiar~ɾɨ ia makahiʦu musuʔɨvɨ tasiar~ɾɨ misee kimia.
S.FORM: noo cumacʉ'ra kimia ia totasiarʉ ia makahicu musu'ʉvʉ tasiarʉ misee kimia.
S.PHON: noo ʦumaʦɨʔr~ɾa kimia ia totasiar~ɾɨ ia makahiʦu musuʔɨvɨ tasiar~ɾɨ misee kimia.
S.TRANSL: 早上看到我們的時候,就說musu'ʉvʉ tasiarʉ.
When seeing us in the morning, He would say, "Good morning."

S.FORM: marivarikim musu'ʉvʉ tasiarʉ misee.
S.PHON: mar~ɾivar~ɾikim musuʔɨvɨ tasiar~ɾɨ misee.
S.FORM: marivarikim musu'ʉvʉ tasiarʉ misee.
S.PHON: mar~ɾivar~ɾikim musuʔɨvɨ tasiar~ɾɨ misee.
S.TRANSL: 我們回答說:「早安」.
We replied, "Good morning."

S.FORM: eh pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉkim mamanʉngana mokusa na taarisinita tavarʉ'ʉ tumututuru kimia marisinatʉ paracani.
S.PHON: eh paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋɨkim mamanɨŋana mokusa na taar~ɾisinita tavar~ɾɨʔɨ tumututur~ɾu kimia mar~ɾisinatɨ par~ɾaʦani.
S.FORM: eh pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉkim mamanʉngana mokusa na taarisinita tavarʉ'ʉ tumututuru kimia marisinatʉ paracani.
S.PHON: eh paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋɨkim mamanɨŋana mokusa na taar~ɾisinita tavar~ɾɨʔɨ tumututur~ɾu kimia mar~ɾisinatɨ par~ɾaʦani.
S.TRANSL: 我們心想能夠去學校真好啊!可以教我們讀書、唱歌.
We thought how good it was to go to school, which could teach us to sing and dance.

S.FORM: nakai noo itumuru 'umo'uma tamna putukikioia kacauacu seto mesua ka'ancu mosa na taarisinata sii tia moran mamarang a mo'uma.
S.PHON: nakai noo itumur~ɾu ʔumoʔuma tamna putukikioia kaʦauaʦu seto mesua kaʔanʦu mosa na taar~ɾisinata sii tia mor~ɾan mamar~ɾaŋ a moʔuma.
S.FORM: nakai noo itumuru 'umo'uma tamna putukikioia kacauacu seto mesua ka'ancu mosa na taarisinata sii tia moran mamarang a mo'uma.
S.PHON: nakai noo itumur~ɾu ʔumoʔuma tamna putukikioia kaʦauaʦu seto mesua kaʔanʦu mosa na taar~ɾisinata sii tia mor~ɾan mamar~ɾaŋ a moʔuma.
S.TRANSL: 但是當農事忙碌的時候,就很多學生不去學校,因為要幫忙長輩們做農事.
But when the farming business was busy, many students wouldn't go to school because they needed to help their seniors do farming.

S.FORM: noo takutavarʉ'ʉ kosicinse isua mokusacu na taira mokusa na 'umo'umamia tikirim seto acuu nankamu misen 'an iovutu misee.
S.PHON: noo takutavar~ɾɨʔɨ kosiʦinse isua mokusaʦu na tair~ɾa mokusa na ʔumoʔumamia tikir~ɾim seto aʦuu nankamu misen ʔan iovutu misee.
S.FORM: noo takutavarʉ'ʉ kosicinse isua mokusacu na taira mokusa na 'umo'umamia tikirim seto acuu nankamu misen 'an iovutu misee.
S.PHON: noo takutavar~ɾɨʔɨ kosiʦinse isua mokusaʦu na tair~ɾa mokusa na ʔumoʔumamia tikir~ɾim seto aʦuu nankamu misen ʔan iovutu misee.
S.TRANSL: 當那位校長獲知之後,就去曠野,到我們的田裡找學生說:「你們去哪裡了,為什麼不來?」

TRANSL.notes: When the principal knew it (students' doing farming), he would go to the open field. (He would) go to our farmland and find the students, saying "Where did you go? Why didn't you come?"

S.TRANSL: When the principal knew it , he would go to the open field. go to our farmland and find the students, saying "Where did you go? Why didn't you come?"

S.FORM: matisa'ʉkimia ah avicin mavici ʉngcoo oh manu tati'ing umavici na taarisinita.
S.PHON: matisaʔɨkimia ah aviʦin maviʦi ɨŋʦoo oh manu tatiʔiŋ umaviʦi na taar~ɾisinita.
S.FORM: matisa'ʉkimia ah avicin mavici ʉngcoo oh manu tati'ing umavici na taarisinita.
S.PHON: matisaʔɨkimia ah aviʦin maviʦi ɨŋʦoo oh manu tatiʔiŋ umaviʦi na taar~ɾisinita.
S.TRANSL: 抓著我們喔,帶著小朋友到學校.

TRANSL.notes: grabbing us (the little kids), he brought the little kids to school.

S.TRANSL: grabbing us , he brought the little kids to school.

S.FORM: 'apanapa arakuracʉ mamarangmia misen cau makasia.
S.PHON: ʔapanapa ar~ɾakur~ɾaʦɨ mamar~ɾaŋmia misen ʦau makasia.
S.FORM: 'apanapa arakuracʉ mamarangmia misen cau makasia.
S.PHON: ʔapanapa ar~ɾakur~ɾaʦɨ mamar~ɾaŋmia misen ʦau makasia.
S.TRANSL: 我們的長輩們有時候會很生氣,怎麼會這樣啊?
Our seniors got very angry from time to time. How would it be so?

S.FORM: tia moran kimia manu putukikio avicinkee mokusa na tarisinita.
S.PHON: tia mor~ɾan kimia manu putukikio aviʦinkee mokusa na tar~ɾisinita.
S.FORM: tia moran kimia manu putukikio avicinkee mokusa na tarisinita.
S.PHON: tia mor~ɾan kimia manu putukikio aviʦinkee mokusa na tar~ɾisinita.
S.TRANSL: 小朋友要幫我們工作,還把小孩子帶去學校.

TRANSL.notes: The little kids would help us work. Should (he) take the kids to school.

S.TRANSL: The little kids would help us work. Should take the kids to school.

S.FORM: makasicu ʉngcoo cang siaucang isua parisinitoo manumu kan tia arakaranananguain tam nguani tamna cʉpʉng miana.
S.PHON: makasiʦu ɨŋʦoo ʦaŋ siauʦaŋ isua par~ɾisinitoo manumu kan tia ar~ɾakar~ɾananaŋuain tam ŋuani tamna ʦɨpɨŋ miana.
S.FORM: makasicu ʉngcoo cang siaucang isua parisinitoo manumu kan tia arakaranananguain tam nguani tamna cʉpʉng miana.
S.PHON: makasiʦu ɨŋʦoo ʦaŋ siauʦaŋ isua par~ɾisinitoo manumu kan tia ar~ɾakar~ɾananaŋuain tam ŋuani tamna ʦɨpɨŋ miana.
S.TRANSL: 那位張校長就如此說:要讓你們的小孩讀書,他們未來的想法會改變的.
That Principal Jhang said in this way, "Let your children go to school and their thoughts will change in the future."

S.FORM: tia aramamanʉng ikamu tamna siacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: tia ar~ɾamamanɨŋ ikamu tamna siaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.FORM: tia aramamanʉng ikamu tamna siacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: tia ar~ɾamamanɨŋ ikamu tamna siaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.TRANSL: 你們的生活方式會變好的.
Your ways of living will become good.

S.FORM: makasuacu macasʉcu niarakukunmia takutavarʉ'ʉ mamarangmia.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu maʦasɨʦu niar~ɾakukunmia takutavar~ɾɨʔɨ mamar~ɾaŋmia.
S.FORM: makasuacu macasʉcu niarakukunmia takutavarʉ'ʉ mamarangmia.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu maʦasɨʦu niar~ɾakukunmia takutavar~ɾɨʔɨ mamar~ɾaŋmia.

TRANSL.notes: 就那樣,我們相處久了,我們的長輩們也了解(那位校長)了.

S.TRANSL: 就那樣,我們相處久了,我們的長輩們也了解 了.

TRANSL.notes: In that way, we got along for so long. Our seniors also understood (that principal).

S.TRANSL: In that way, we got along for so long. Our seniors also understood .

S.FORM: cucuruava mokusa na taarisinata mamanmita niarakaranana ti'ingcu tavarʉ'ʉcu sumasʉpʉrʉ ah ti'ing tavarʉ'ʉ ti'ing masii Nakasaiana.
S.PHON: ʦuʦur~ɾuava mokusa na taar~ɾisinata mamanmita niar~ɾakar~ɾanana tiʔiŋʦu tavar~ɾɨʔɨʦu sumasɨpɨr~ɾɨ ah tiʔiŋ tavar~ɾɨʔɨ tiʔiŋ masii nakasaiana.
S.FORM: cucuruava mokusa na taarisinata mamanmita niarakaranana ti'ingcu tavarʉ'ʉcu sumasʉpʉrʉ ah ti'ing tavarʉ'ʉ ti'ing masii Nakasaiana.
S.PHON: ʦuʦur~ɾuava mokusa na taar~ɾisinata mamanmita niar~ɾakar~ɾanana tiʔiŋʦu tavar~ɾɨʔɨʦu sumasɨpɨr~ɾɨ ah tiʔiŋ tavar~ɾɨʔɨ tiʔiŋ masii nakasaiana.
S.TRANSL: 的確,我們的小朋友去了學校之後,稍微有點改變喔,會念一點中文.

TRANSL.notes: Truly, after our little kids went to school, (They) changed slightly, being able to read a little Chinese.

S.TRANSL: Truly, after our little kids went to school, changed slightly, being able to read a little Chinese.

S.FORM: makasuacu pairacu nguain rorovana ia mokusa na tanasʉn arakukun mamarang parʉ'ʉna.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu pair~ɾaʦu ŋuain r~ɾor~ɾovana ia mokusa na tanasɨn ar~ɾakukun mamar~ɾaŋ par~ɾɨʔɨna.
S.FORM: makasuacu pairacu nguain rorovana ia mokusa na tanasʉn arakukun mamarang parʉ'ʉna.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu pair~ɾaʦu ŋuain r~ɾor~ɾovana ia mokusa na tanasɨn ar~ɾakukun mamar~ɾaŋ par~ɾɨʔɨna.
S.TRANSL: 之後,晚上的時候,他經常來到社區,跟老人家相處聊天.
Afterwards, he often came to the community at night and accompanied the seniors, chatting with them.

S.FORM: ah macasʉ taniarʉ makaicu nguain mamarangmia kinvavarangmia arakukun kimia kavang cumacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: ah maʦasɨ taniar~ɾɨ makaiʦu ŋuain mamar~ɾaŋmia kinvavar~ɾaŋmia ar~ɾakukun kimia kavaŋ ʦumaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.FORM: ah macasʉ taniarʉ makaicu nguain mamarangmia kinvavarangmia arakukun kimia kavang cumacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: ah maʦasɨ taniar~ɾɨ makaiʦu ŋuain mamar~ɾaŋmia kinvavar~ɾaŋmia ar~ɾakukun kimia kavaŋ ʦumaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.TRANSL: 他就像我們的長輩,像我們的兄弟姊妹們,跟我們在一起生活.
He's just like our senior relatives and our siblings, living with us.

W.TRANSL: relation-重疊-兄弟姊妹=1PL.屬格.排除

S.FORM: imo mesua arakacauacu seto mamancu mokusa na taarisinita arakukun nia mon nia marisinitʉ.
S.PHON: imo mesua ar~ɾakaʦauaʦu seto mamanʦu mokusa na taar~ɾisinita ar~ɾakukun nia mon nia mar~ɾisinitɨ.
S.FORM: imo mesua arakacauacu seto mamancu mokusa na taarisinita arakukun nia mon nia marisinitʉ.
S.PHON: imo mesua ar~ɾakaʦauaʦu seto mamanʦu mokusa na taar~ɾisinita ar~ɾakukun nia mon nia mar~ɾisinitɨ.
S.TRANSL: 所以,當時很多學生變成喜歡去學校跟他相處,在那裡讀書.
So, many students got to like going to the school to get along with him and study there.

S.FORM: makasuacu 'unacu cau mokusa na namanamarʉ marisinitʉ aratatia nguain tamna takutavara'a araitumuru nguain tamna takutavara'a.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu ʔunaʦu ʦau mokusa na namanamar~ɾɨ mar~ɾisinitɨ ar~ɾatatia ŋuain tamna takutavar~ɾaʔa ar~ɾaitumur~ɾu ŋuain tamna takutavar~ɾaʔa.
S.FORM: makasuacu 'unacu cau mokusa na namanamarʉ marisinitʉ aratatia nguain tamna takutavara'a araitumuru nguain tamna takutavara'a.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu ʔunaʦu ʦau mokusa na namanamar~ɾɨ mar~ɾisinitɨ ar~ɾatatia ŋuain tamna takutavar~ɾaʔa ar~ɾaitumur~ɾu ŋuain tamna takutavar~ɾaʔa.
S.TRANSL: 之後,有人去平地讀書,懂得更多了.
Afterwards, some people went to the plain area for study, knowing much more.

S.FORM: poi'i na tanasʉmia moran cau pakamamanʉng.
S.PHON: poiʔi na tanasɨmia mor~ɾan ʦau pakamamanɨŋ.
S.FORM: poi'i na tanasʉmia moran cau pakamamanʉng.
S.PHON: poiʔi na tanasɨmia mor~ɾan ʦau pakamamanɨŋ.
S.TRANSL: 回到部落協助族人更好.

TRANSL.notes: (They) returned to the tribe, helping the tribal men lead a better life.

S.TRANSL: returned to the tribe, helping the tribal men lead a better life.

S.FORM: pacʉpʉcʉcʉngmia ah 'unava kocisinse makasia tumatuturu kimia pakamamanʉng tanasʉmia.
S.PHON: paʦɨpɨʦɨʦɨŋmia ah ʔunava koʦisinse makasia tumatutur~ɾu kimia pakamamanɨŋ tanasɨmia.
S.FORM: pacʉpʉcʉcʉngmia ah 'unava kocisinse makasia tumatuturu kimia pakamamanʉng tanasʉmia.
S.PHON: paʦɨpɨʦɨʦɨŋmia ah ʔunava koʦisinse makasia tumatutur~ɾu kimia pakamamanɨŋ tanasɨmia.
S.TRANSL: 我們心想著,啊,原來有這樣的校長,教我們使我們的部落更好.
We thought "Ah, there turns out to be such a principal who teaches us to make our tribe better."

S.FORM: imo miaranaucu kocisinse iisi aratʉkʉn na tanasʉn niaraaka kacaua cau ikim kanakanavu pacʉpʉcʉpʉng 'inia.
S.PHON: imo miar~ɾanauʦu koʦisinse iisi ar~ɾatɨkɨn na tanasɨn niar~ɾaaka kaʦaua ʦau ikim kanakanavu paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋ ʔinia.
S.FORM: imo miaranaucu kocisinse iisi aratʉkʉn na tanasʉn niaraaka kacaua cau ikim kanakanavu pacʉpʉcʉpʉng 'inia.
S.PHON: imo miar~ɾanauʦu koʦisinse iisi ar~ɾatɨkɨn na tanasɨn niar~ɾaaka kaʦaua ʦau ikim kanakanavu paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋ ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 所以,雖然這位校長離開部落很久了也過世了,我們Kanakanavu族人還是很思念著他.
So, though the principal has left the tribe for a long time, we Kanakanavu still miss him a lot.

S.FORM: tiavakita ketuturu 'inia makasua moran na cau pakamamunʉng na cau masii aramamanʉng kangvang cau tamna siacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: tiavakita ketutur~ɾu ʔinia makasua mor~ɾan na ʦau pakamamunɨŋ na ʦau masii ar~ɾamamanɨŋ kaŋvaŋ ʦau tamna siaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.FORM: tiavakita ketuturu 'inia makasua moran na cau pakamamunʉng na cau masii aramamanʉng kangvang cau tamna siacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: tiavakita ketutur~ɾu ʔinia makasua mor~ɾan na ʦau pakamamunɨŋ na ʦau masii ar~ɾamamanɨŋ kaŋvaŋ ʦau tamna siaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.TRANSL: 我們原來要學他那種幫助別人、讓別人好的作為,才能使族人的生活都獲得改善.

TRANSL.notes: It is the case that we learn the very way he helped others and made others live well. In so doing, (he) could improve the lives of the tribal men.

S.TRANSL: It is the case that we learn the very way he helped others and made others live well. In so doing, could improve the lives of the tribal men.

S.FORM: matarava masin ikim Kanakanavu noo parʉ'ʉna poisua kocisinse cangsiaucang ia 'esi pakim pacʉpʉcʉpʉng 'inia.
S.PHON: matar~ɾava masin ikim kanakanavu noo par~ɾɨʔɨna poisua koʦisinse ʦaŋsiauʦaŋ ia ʔesi pakim paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋ ʔinia.
S.FORM: matarava masin ikim Kanakanavu noo parʉ'ʉna poisua kocisinse cangsiaucang ia 'esi pakim pacʉpʉcʉpʉng 'inia.
S.PHON: matar~ɾava masin ikim kanakanavu noo par~ɾɨʔɨna poisua koʦisinse ʦaŋsiauʦaŋ ia ʔesi pakim paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋ ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 直到現在我們Kanakanavu族人聊到張校長的時候,我們還在思念著他.
Up to now, whenever we Kanakanavu talk about Principal Zhang, we still miss him.

S.FORM: 'una kari makasi sua cangsiaucang kan ia Kanakanavu tamna kocisinse matarava nuari.
S.PHON: ʔuna kar~ɾi makasi sua ʦaŋsiauʦaŋ kan ia kanakanavu tamna koʦisinse matar~ɾava nuar~ɾi.
S.FORM: 'una kari makasi sua cangsiaucang kan ia Kanakanavu tamna kocisinse matarava nuari.
S.PHON: ʔuna kar~ɾi makasi sua ʦaŋsiauʦaŋ kan ia kanakanavu tamna koʦisinse matar~ɾava nuar~ɾi.
S.TRANSL: 所以,有這麼一說,張校長是我們Kanakanavu族人永遠的校長.
Therefore, it is said that Principal Zhang is our Kanakanavu's principal for good.
